name: Bolt Mono
explanation: A lean, punchy two-oscillator hard-sync mono synth in the compact American two-VCO lead/bass tradition. OSC-1 is a saw master; OSC-2 is a detuned pulse that hard-syncs to the master, resetting its phase every master cycle for the signature zappy, metallic sync edge. Both feed a punchy Moog-style 4-pole resonant ladder low-pass driven by a fast decay envelope, with quick glide and a snappy amp envelope. Sync adds the aggressive edge, Cutoff and Resonance snap, Detune fattens slightly, and the whole voice stays tight, fast and immediate — a small, aggressive lead/bass machine. Pure algorithm, no samples; pitch tracks each played note in Hz.
